text: The beginning of the year would be either Tishri (in the autumn), for the civil calendar year, or Nisan (in the spring), for the sacred calendar year. The tenth of the month of Tishri is Yom Kippur, a significant event in the Jewish religious calendar.
In other words, the length of each cubit was the usual cubit with one palm width added, also called the royal cubit.
A reed is six cubits, and each cubit is the royal cubit, which is longer by one palm width than the usual cubit.
